content:
# Maximum Flooding Surface

**Summary**: A surface representing a period of maximum transgression.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015]] identified maximum-flooding-surface to define the boundaries between sequences.

- [[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024]] identified the J40 Maximum Flooding Surface (MFS) using planktic and deep-water indicators.

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015]] Key markers for identifying transgressive systems tracts.

- [[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024]] identified the J40 Maximum Flooding Surface (MFS) using planktic and deep-water indicators.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015]] identified via log peaks and seismic attributes

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Three maximum flooding surfaces (MFS1, MFS2, and MFS3) were identified. ([[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015, p. 17]])
- [[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015]] MFS1, MFS2, MFS3

- The planktic taxon (Globuligerina sp.) and deep-water indicators (Gerochammina n.sp. and Bulbobaculites n.sp.) serve as critical markers for recognizing the J40 MFS. ([[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024, p. 16]])
